name: Black Forest Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Morello cherry syrup or sour cherry syrup
1 cup whipping cream, well-chilled (heavy whipping cream)
recipeInstructions:
Cherry Topping:
1. Soak cherries and kirsch in a small bowl for 6 hours.
2. Drain cherries through a strainer into a medium bowl, shaking occasionally, for at least two hours.
3. Measure enough Morello cherry syrup to add the cherry liquid to 1 cup. Pour into an 8-inch skillet (reserve remaining liquid). Halve cherries and add them. Cook until thickened and resembling preserves, about 6 minutes.
Chocolate Crust:
1. Generously butter a 9-inch springform pan.
2. Finely crush cookies in a processor using on/off turns.
3. Cut in butter with on/off turns until mixture begins to gather together.
4. Press crumbs into the bottom of the pan and up the sides to 3/4 inch from the top.
5. Refrigerate crust for at least 30 minutes.
Chocolate Filling:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. In a heavy medium saucepan, heat cream with chocolate until melted, stirring constantly.
3. Cool for 10 minutes.
4. Beat cream cheese with sugar until smooth.
5. Beat in eggs one at a time until just combined.
6. Add chocolate mixture and remaining cherry liquid with vanilla to the cream mixture.
7. Pour into the crust.
8. Bake until outer 2 inches are firm but center still moves slightly, about 1 1/4 hours. Cool completely on a rack.
9. Cover top with foil and refrigerate for 1-2 days.
Top:
1. Spread cherry topping over the cake.
2. Beat remaining cream with sugar and kirsch until peaks form.
3. Spoon into the center of the cake.
4. Top with chocolate curls if desired.
5. Let stand at room temperature for 15 minutes before serving.
